User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in AcademiaFlow, storing basic profile information and personalized settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification.

isPro.description: Indicates if the user has a pro subscription.

defaultStudyDurationMinutes.description: The user's default duration for study sessions, in minutes.

defaultBreakDurationMinutes.description: The user's default duration for breaks, in minutes.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Subject.title: Subject
Subject.description: Represents an academic subject or topic a user studies, allowing for categorization of study sessions and notes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subject entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this subject. (Relationship: User 1:N Subject)

name.description: The name of the subject, e.g., 'Calculus I' or 'World History'.

description.description: An optional description or brief overview of the subject.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subject was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subject was last updated.

StudySession.title: StudySession
StudySession.description: Records details of a single study session, including its duration, associated subject, and any notes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudySession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who conducted this study session. (Relationship: User 1:N StudySession)

subjectId.description: Reference to the Subject this session was focused on. (Relationship: Subject 1:N StudySession)

startTime.description: The precise start time of the study session.

endTime.description: The precise end time of the study session.

durationMinutes.description: The total duration of the study session in minutes.

notes.description: Short notes or key takeaways from this specific study session.

aiFocusQuestion.description: The focused question or sub-topic suggested by the AI for this session, if used.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the study session record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the study session record was last updated.

Note.title: Note
Note.description: Represents a user's general or session-specific note, designed to support the 'notebooks pages' feature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Note entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created this note. (Relationship: User 1:N Note)

subjectId.description: Optional reference to the Subject this note is associated with. (Relationship: Subject 1:N Note)

studySessionId.description: Optional reference to the StudySession this note pertains to. (Relationship: StudySession 1:N Note)

content.description: The main text content of the note.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the note was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the note was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) must match `request.auth.uid` for direct user ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

definition.description: Stores academic subjects created by a specific user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this subject.

params.name: subjectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific subject.

definition.description: Records details of study sessions conducted by a user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who conducted this study session.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific study session.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who created this note.

params.name: noteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific note.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed around strict user-based ownership to ensure high security and easy queryability.
